content.problemStatement:
Advanced Features
- Multiple Index Levels: You can use multiple columns for the index, creating a hierarchical index.
- Multiple Value Columns: You can aggregate multiple value columns in the same pivot table.
- Using a Dictionary for Aggregation: This allows you to apply different aggregation functions to different columns.
- Adding Margins: The margins=True parameter adds row and column totals to your pivot table.
- Filling Missing Values: The fill_value parameter allows you to replace NaN values with a specified value.

Check out the implementation of these features in the IDE - click on 'Submit' to run the code.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
